title: Bundled Core Review Practice Questions
notes: Original practice questions for internal deck generation. These are not official ABR items.

questions.stem: A resident identifies a near miss in which the wrong prior examination was almost compared before finalizing a report. Which response best supports a just-culture review?

options.text: Issue a private warning to the resident and close the event without discussion
Conduct a de-identified review that looks for system contributors and shares lessons with the team
Avoid documenting the event because no patient harm occurred
Report the resident to the credentialing committee before any local review

questions.explanation: Near misses are most useful when reviewed in a structured, non-punitive way that looks for latent system causes and opportunities for safer workflow design.

questions.stem: A chest radiograph obtained in the emergency department shows a new tension pneumothorax. What is the best next step for result communication?

options.text: Finalize the report promptly and rely on the electronic inbox
Send a routine secure message to the ordering team
Directly contact the responsible clinician immediately and document the communication
Wait for a second attending to confirm the interpretation before notifying anyone

questions.explanation: Unexpected, potentially life-threatening findings require direct, timely communication plus documentation that the message reached a responsible clinician.

questions.stem: A patient reports a prior intracranial aneurysm clip, but no operative record or implant card is available. Which action is most appropriate before MRI?

options.text: Proceed if the patient has previously undergone CT without issue
Proceed using a low-field-strength magnet only
Delay the MRI until implant compatibility is verified through reliable documentation
Proceed if a screening form was signed by the patient

questions.explanation: Unknown implant compatibility is an MRI safety issue. Reliable documentation is needed before the patient enters the scanner environment.

questions.stem: A department wants to track a process measure for a quality-improvement project focused on emergency department radiology workflow. Which metric is a process measure?

options.text: Mean time from image completion to final report for emergency department CT studies
Five-year hospital mortality for all emergency department patients
Regional population life expectancy
Annual hospital operating margin

questions.explanation: Process measures track how care is delivered. Turnaround time is a workflow metric that can be measured repeatedly as the intervention is refined.

questions.stem: A patient develops limited urticaria shortly after iodinated contrast administration but remains hemodynamically stable without respiratory symptoms. Which immediate management is most appropriate?

options.text: Recognize a mild reaction, assess for progression, and provide symptomatic treatment or observation as needed
Initiate chest compressions immediately
Send the patient home without assessment because hives are always self-limited
Assume this is vasovagal syncope and place the patient in Trendelenburg position

questions.explanation: Limited urticaria without cardiopulmonary compromise is typically managed as a mild contrast reaction with observation and symptom-based treatment.

questions.stem: Which workflow change most directly reduces the risk of reports being finalized on the wrong patient or wrong study?

options.text: Suppress patient identifiers on the reporting screen to reduce clutter
Require active verification of patient and accession information before final signature
Allow dictation to continue even after a study is switched in the PACS viewer
Shorten all reports so fewer details are available for review

questions.explanation: High-reliability workflow design uses deliberate patient-and-study verification steps at the point of interpretation and finalization.

questions.stem: If the distance from an x-ray source to a point in space is doubled, what happens to the exposure at that point, assuming all other factors are unchanged?

options.text: It doubles
It stays the same
It falls to one half
It falls to one quarter

questions.explanation: Exposure follows the inverse square law, so doubling distance reduces exposure by a factor of four.

questions.stem: In helical CT, increasing pitch while keeping the other major exposure settings unchanged generally has what effect on patient dose?

options.text: Dose increases
Dose decreases
Dose becomes unpredictable
Dose is unchanged by pitch

questions.explanation: A higher pitch spreads the beam over a longer z-axis distance per rotation, which generally reduces dose when other parameters are held constant.

questions.stem: Susceptibility artifact is typically most conspicuous on which MRI sequence family?

options.text: Spin echo
Gradient echo
Inversion recovery
Fast spin echo with short echo train

questions.explanation: Gradient-echo sequences are especially sensitive to susceptibility effects because they lack the 180-degree refocusing pulse that reduces dephasing.

questions.stem: Color Doppler aliasing is present. Which adjustment most directly reduces aliasing?

options.text: Lower the pulse repetition frequency
Increase the pulse repetition frequency
Increase transducer contact pressure only
Switch from color Doppler to B-mode without changing any settings

questions.explanation: Raising the pulse repetition frequency increases the Nyquist limit and is one of the standard ways to reduce Doppler aliasing.

questions.stem: A radiotracer has an activity of 20 mCi and a physical half-life of 6 hours. Assuming only physical decay, what activity remains after 12 hours? State the answer in mCi.
questions.explanation: Twelve hours is two half-lives, so 20 mCi decreases to 10 mCi and then to 5 mCi.

questions.stem: What does the half-value layer of an x-ray beam represent?

options.text: The tube current that doubles photon output
The absorber thickness that reduces beam intensity to one half
The anode angle that eliminates heel effect
The detector exposure needed to avoid quantum mottle

questions.explanation: Half-value layer is the thickness of specified absorbing material required to reduce x-ray beam intensity to 50 percent of its original value.
